spec.name: Replace with next/image in 2 photo components, add Supabase remotePatterns to next.config.ts, and add server-side validatePhotoFile call in uploadPhoto server action

story.name: Replace with next/image in 2 photo components, add Supabase remotePatterns to next.config.ts, and add server-side validatePhotoFile call in uploadPhoto server action

relevant_decisions.decision: Inclusive-day semantics for planned_start/planned_end (end = start + duration − 1)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-TM-2 says "planned_end = planned_start + 2 days" for a 2-day task. Two interpretations: exclusive (start=06-01, end=06-03 → 2 nights) or inclusive (start=06-01, end=06-02 → first and last working day, 2 working days).
alternatives_considered: Exclusive: planned_end = planned_start + duration_days. Simpler arithmetic but a 1-day task would render as a zero-width Gantt bar and the end date is a day the task is NOT worked.
Inclusive: planned_end = planned_start + (duration_days − 1). Matches how humans read a schedule ("Monday–Tuesday = 2 days") and matches the existing seed data pattern.
relevant_decisions.rationale: Checked the seeded O'Brien data + the template-apply cursor schedule — both treat planned_start and planned_end as the first and last working day of the task (inclusive). A 1-day task has planned_start = planned_end. The cursor schedule in lib/templates/apply.ts advances via `start = prev.planned_end + 1`, which is exactly AC-TM-4's "max(dep.planned_end) + 1 day" formula — consistent across the codebase.

relevant_decisions.decision: AC-TM-6 dependency cleanup relies on ON DELETE CASCADE on task_dependencies.depends_on_task_id
relevant_decisions.context: The spec requires that deleting task A removes it from the depends_on array of every downstream task. Options: handle in application code, use the DB cascade, or both.
alternatives_considered: Application-layer: SELECT dependents, DELETE by task_id = A, then DELETE rows where depends_on_task_id = A. Three round trips, race conditions if another request is editing deps simultaneously.
DB cascade only: task_dependencies has ON DELETE CASCADE on BOTH foreign keys (task_id, depends_on_task_id). Deleting task A atomically removes every row that references A in either column.
Both layers for defense in depth: duplicates effort without improving guarantees.
relevant_decisions.rationale: The schema already guarantees AC-TM-6 via CASCADE. Server action just deletes the task and RLS + CASCADE do the rest. Added a pure removeFromDependencies() helper for the UI to mirror the same behaviour in client state — so the task list re-renders correctly without a round trip.

relevant_decisions.decision: updateTaskDuration holds planned_start fixed and recalculates only planned_end
relevant_decisions.context: AC-TM-5 says "edits the duration_days field and saves, THEN the task's planned_end recalculates and cascades." Options: recompute planned_start from deps then extend end, or keep start stable and extend/shrink end.
alternatives_considered: Recompute planned_start from deps on every duration edit — could shift the start date if deps have changed since task creation, surprising the user when they just edited duration.
Hold start fixed, shift end only — matches the AC's "end recalculates" wording and is what cascade_task_dates() is designed for (it receives new_planned_end and propagates the delta downstream).
Re-run the full schedule — massive blast radius for a single edit.
relevant_decisions.rationale: cascade_task_dates(p_task_id, p_new_planned_start, p_new_planned_end) expects the caller to pick the new end. Passing the current planned_start unchanged + new planned_end = start + (duration − 1) gives a clean delta the recursive CTE uses to shift every downstream task by the same offset. One RPC, one transaction, O(downstream) updates.

relevant_decisions.decision: Add Task lives on /stages/:id, reached via an "Open" link on each stage row in /schedule
relevant_decisions.context: AC-TM-1 says "clicks Add Task within [a stage]". Three possible homes: inline on /schedule (expand each stage), dedicated /stages/:id detail, or a full modal launched from the schedule list.
alternatives_considered: Inline-on-schedule: every stage on the schedule page expands into a task list. Crowds a Gantt-like screen; doesn't scale past ~3 tasks per stage.
/stages/:id detail page with full task CRUD: clean single-purpose screen, future-proof for per-stage editing, and the /stages/:id route already exists as a placeholder.
Global /tasks list: loses the stage scoping AC-TM-1 requires.
relevant_decisions.rationale: The foundation already scaffolded /stages/[id]/page.tsx as a placeholder route and the /schedule StageManager already has per-stage rows. Adding an "Open" link to each row + rewriting the stage detail page as the task-CRUD screen uses existing infrastructure, keeps /schedule lightweight, and gives tasks a natural home for future attributes (materials, photos, trade assignment).

relevant_decisions.decision: Trade assignment on /tasks/:id is an inline optimistic <select>, not a modal
relevant_decisions.context: AC-TR-2 says 'assigns it to a task via task edit form.' The existing Add Task form already has a trade dropdown; the detail screen needs a standalone control for re-assigning an existing task.
alternatives_considered: Open a full edit-task modal from the detail page — duplicates StageTasksManager's add form, and this story's scope is trade-contacts not task-edit.
Inline 'Edit' button that opens an edit drawer with every task field — scope creep into task-management follow-up work already flagged in factory_questions.
Dedicated /tasks/:id/edit route — extra navigation step for a single-field change.
relevant_decisions.rationale: A single <select> element on the detail page changes trade_id in-place with an optimistic client update + server rollback on error. Matches the lightweight direct-manipulation pattern from StageManager's drag-reorder and avoids opening a form just to change one field. Other fields (name/notes/duration) are explicitly deferred to a future task-edit story per the existing factory_question.

relevant_decisions.decision: Materials live inline on /tasks/:id as a TaskMaterialsManager section (not a dedicated /materials/:id route)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-ML-1 says 'clicks Add Material on the task detail screen, THEN an inline form appears'. Affected screens also list /materials but no AC describes what belongs there. Choice: inline section, modal, or dedicated subroute.
alternatives_considered: Modal dialog like the trade add/edit flow — heavier UI for what the spec calls 'inline form'
Dedicated /tasks/:id/materials subroute — extra navigation step, breaks the 'everything about this task on one screen' mental model
Leave on the /materials list page — contradicts AC-ML-1's 'task detail screen' wording
relevant_decisions.rationale: A section within /tasks/:id with an inline expandable form matches AC-ML-1 literally, keeps the task as the container for its materials (which is how the data model is scoped: materials.task_id NOT NULL), and reuses the existing TaskTradeAssigner layout on that page. The /materials index remains the future home for a cross-task materials dashboard (ordering, deliveries, inventory) — that belongs to a separate story.

relevant_decisions.decision: Cascade summary layer is a pure TS module over the existing PG function
relevant_decisions.context: cascade_task_dates() was already implemented as a plpgsql recursive-CTE function by the initial schema migration. It owns the transactional update (AC-CE-1, AC-CE-2, AC-CE-5) and returns downstream changes only. AC-CE-3 requires a summary listing ALL tasks that moved — including the trigger task — with old and new dates.
alternatives_considered: Rewrite cascade_task_dates to include the trigger task in its RETURN TABLE — would require a new SQL migration and coordinate with the existing callers (customization.ts, materials/operations.ts)
Compute the full summary inside the server action ad-hoc — duplicates logic across updateTaskDuration and any future date-drag UI
Ship the downstream-only result to clients and have the UI prepend the trigger — mixes presentation with transaction shape, hard to unit-test
relevant_decisions.rationale: lib/cascade/engine.ts is a pure module: computeCascadeDelta, shiftTaskDates, buildCascadeSummary. 13 unit tests covering delta math, month-boundaries, zero-downstream case (AC-CE-4), trigger-as-first-row, and identical delta on every row. Server actions call buildCascadeSummary with (trigger row from pre-update SELECT, downstream rows from RPC return). Zero SQL churn, testable without a DB.

relevant_decisions.decision: Delay validation rejects same-date and earlier dates (forward-only push)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DL-2 says 'user selects a later date'. Ambiguous on equality: does a same-date apply count as a valid no-op? Also unclear whether pulling the date in (earlier) counts as delay logging.
alternatives_considered: Accept same-date as a no-op + show a 'No changes' toast — wastes a cascade RPC round trip and confuses the user about whether the delay was recorded
Accept any date (earlier or later) — conflates delay logging with duration editing, which already exists as updateTaskDuration and handles pull-in semantics
Silently ignore equal dates on the client — hides the validation failure and disables the Apply button without explanation
relevant_decisions.rationale: validateDelayDate requires strictly newPlannedEnd > currentPlannedEnd. The Apply button disables with an inline error ('New date must be after the current end date') when the user picks the same or an earlier date. Forward-only matches the user mental model of 'log a delay' and keeps the feature orthogonal to updateTaskDuration (which handles both forward and backward duration changes).

relevant_decisions.decision: Materials-moved count derived post-cascade by counting materials on affected task ids
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DL-3 requires the overlay to show M material order-by dates moved. cascade_task_dates() updates material order_by_date in-place but its RETURN TABLE only reports downstream TASKS, not materials. Options: (a) rewrite the RPC to return material counts, (b) count materials attached to affected tasks in a follow-up SELECT, (c) compute in the RSC on next page load.
alternatives_considered: Modify the PG function to return material_count — new SQL migration, breaks existing updateTaskDuration callers that expect only downstream task rows
Render the count on the next /briefing load — the overlay must be immediate per AC-DL-3, cross-navigation is a different UX
Track material updates via a DB trigger logging to shift_alerts — shift_alerts population is deferred to the daily-briefing cycle per existing factory_question
relevant_decisions.rationale: After cascade_task_dates returns the downstream task ids, a single SELECT COUNT on materials WHERE task_id IN (trigger + downstream) gives M. The PG function updates exactly those materials (see initial_schema.sql lines 459-485), so the count is precise. One extra round trip, no SQL churn, no coordination with updateTaskDuration callers.

relevant_decisions.decision: Delay picker lives on /tasks/:id as an inline expandable section, not a modal or /briefing button
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DL-1 says the picker opens from /tasks/:id OR /briefing. /briefing currently renders Upcoming Orders only (no task list). Options: inline section on /tasks/:id, modal dialog, add a tasks section to /briefing, or both.
alternatives_considered: Modal dialog — heavier UI for a single-field form; matches the cascade summary overlay but doubles the overlay layers
Add a Today's Tasks section to /briefing with a Log Delay button per row — expands the briefing scope beyond what AC-DL-1 requires and overlaps with a future daily-briefing story
Combined inline + briefing buttons — duplicates UI surface and doubles the QA matrix
relevant_decisions.rationale: AC-DL-1 wording is OR — one location satisfies it. /tasks/:id is the natural home: the task's other per-task controls (trade assigner, materials manager) already live there, the planned_end is already shown in the header, and an inline expandable section matches the in-place-edit pattern from TaskTradeAssigner. A Log Delay button on /briefing can be added in a follow-up briefing story when the tasks section is designed.

relevant_decisions.decision: Builder-defined acceptance criteria for cascade-material-sync (spec was empty)
relevant_decisions.context: state.json story entry for cascade-material-sync has no acceptance_criteria field — only name, affected_entities (Material), affected_screens (/materials, /briefing), depends_on (order-by-calculation, cascade-engine). story_context.spec was just {id, name}. Builder phase prompt says: ambiguity → check context, make decision, log as factory_decision.
alternatives_considered: Block the story with a factory_question asking a human to write ACs — defeats the autonomous loop, and the story name + screens are specific enough to synthesize a coherent contract.
Inherit cascade-engine ACs (AC-CE-*) — those are about task cascade, not material sync; would duplicate coverage.

Treat the story as already complete (SQL already syncs materials) and skip — ignores the UI side of the "sync" (surfacing which materials moved in the overlay + revalidating /materials and /briefing).
relevant_decisions.rationale: Defined AC-CMS-1 (pure helper mirrors SQL material-update math), AC-CMS-2 (server actions return MaterialMovement[] not just a count), AC-CMS-3 (revalidate /materials on cascade), AC-CMS-4 (revalidate /briefing on cascade). These four criteria directly map to the story name (Cascade Material Order-By Sync) and the two affected_screens. Each AC has tests or a concrete revalidatePath call. QA can verify all four against seeded data.

relevant_decisions.decision: Mirror SQL cascade_task_dates() material math in TypeScript (computeMaterialMovements) rather than read order_by_date back from DB
relevant_decisions.context: The SQL cascade already writes the new order_by_date value in the same transaction. Two ways to surface per-material movements to the client: (a) after RPC, re-SELECT materials — get new value, lose old value; (b) mirror the math in JS using each task old/new planned_start + lead_time_days.
alternatives_considered: Read order_by_date before + after the RPC — two extra round-trips, race with any concurrent cascade.
Extend cascade_task_dates() to RETURN material rows — bigger surface area, needs migration, changes the RPC contract cascade-engine/delay-logging already depend on.
Skip per-material movements, keep the count-only summary — loses fidelity; the user can see which tasks shifted but not which specific materials they need to rush orders for.
relevant_decisions.rationale: computeMaterialMovements is a pure function sharing computeOrderByDate with lib/materials/operations.ts — same math the DB trigger and UI-predict path already use, so the three layers cannot diverge. Server action loads material metadata once (id/name/lead_time/task_id), builds old/new start map from RPC response, and computes both old and new order_by_date locally. No second DB read, no race, testable without mocks. Count of "materials moved" excludes zero-delta rows so the trigger task's own materials (whose planned_start is unchanged in both current cascade paths) do not inflate the number.

relevant_decisions.decision: Cycle detection gates addDependency (existing tasks), not createTask (new tasks)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DV-1 says 'when the user attempts to add A to B's depends_on array'. For a brand-new task with no ID yet, no other task can depend on it, so adding deps to a new task cannot create cycles. The cycle-detection gate belongs on the addDependency server action for existing tasks.
alternatives_considered: Gate createTask with cycle detection — cycles are impossible for new tasks since no other task references them yet. Would add unnecessary DB queries on every task creation.
Only validate in the UI (client-side) — the server must be the authority to prevent bypass via API calls.
relevant_decisions.rationale: addDependency loads all project edges, runs detectCycle (O(V+E) DFS), and rejects with the formatted cycle path. createTask skips the check because the new task is a DAG leaf by definition. Server-side validation prevents bypass.

relevant_decisions.decision: Stale dep cleanup on page load (RSC) rather than a background cron or explicit user action
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DV-2 says 'WHEN the task is loaded, THEN the stale ID is automatically removed'. Options: cleanup on page load, background cron, explicit user action.
alternatives_considered: Background cron job to periodically sweep stale deps — adds infrastructure for a rare scenario, delays cleanup until next sweep
Explicit 'Clean dependencies' button — adds UI surface, requires user awareness of stale deps
relevant_decisions.rationale: The stages/[id] RSC already loads tasks and deps. Running removeStaleDependencies on the loaded edges + deleting stale rows from the DB is zero-latency (same query, no extra round trip for the common case of zero stale deps). Matches AC-DV-2's 'when loaded' trigger literally. ON DELETE CASCADE on task_dependencies already handles most cases, so stale deps are rare — this is a safety net.

relevant_decisions.decision: Available dep targets include ALL project tasks (not just other-stage tasks)
relevant_decisions.context: The original stages/[id] page filtered availableTasks to exclude this stage's tasks. But dependencies within the same stage are valid (e.g., 'Pour foundation' before 'Damp proof course').
alternatives_considered: Keep the cross-stage-only filter — prevents same-stage deps, which are valid and common in construction sequences
Show all tasks including self — the modal filter already excludes the task being edited
relevant_decisions.rationale: Changed availableTasks to include all project tasks. The dep editor modal filters out the task itself (opt.id !== depEditTarget.id). Same-stage deps are the most common case in construction (sequential tasks within a build phase).

relevant_decisions.decision: Quick actions link to /tasks/:id with hash anchors rather than inline modals
relevant_decisions.context: AC-QA-3 specifies Log Delay, Add Note, View Detail as quick actions. Log Delay already exists on /tasks/:id as TaskDelayLogger. Add Note needs a notes-editing UI that does not yet exist.
alternatives_considered: Inline modal per quick action on /briefing — duplicates TaskDelayLogger and creates a new notes editor; bloats briefing client JS
Navigate to /tasks/:id (chosen) — reuses existing per-task controls, keeps briefing lightweight
Bottom sheet on mobile — custom animation + portal complexity for what is a simple navigation
relevant_decisions.rationale: Link to /tasks/:id (with #delay and #notes hash anchors) reuses the existing delay logger on the detail page and avoids building a duplicate. The Add Note action links to #notes which will activate when a notes-editing section is added to the task detail in a future story. View Detail is a plain navigation. Keeps the briefing page focused on its primary role (status overview) rather than becoming a full task-editing surface.

relevant_decisions.decision: Shift alert inserts are best-effort — failure does not block the cascade result
relevant_decisions.context: insertShiftAlerts runs after the cascade RPC succeeds. If the insert fails (e.g. RLS issue, network), the cascade already committed.
alternatives_considered: Wrap cascade + alert insert in a transaction — requires a stored procedure or RPC, adds migration complexity for a non-critical write
Fail the whole action if alerts fail — makes a UX notification more important than the actual data update
Best-effort insert, ignore errors — cascade is the authoritative mutation; alerts are informational
relevant_decisions.rationale: The cascade is the source of truth for dates. Shift alerts are a convenience display. Failing to record an alert is preferable to failing to cascade a date change. The briefing page still renders correctly with or without alerts.

relevant_decisions.decision: Builder-defined acceptance criteria for push-notifications (spec was empty)
relevant_decisions.context: state.json story entry for push-notifications has no acceptance_criteria field — only name, affected_screens (/settings), depends_on (daily-briefing-view). story_context.spec was just {id, name}.
alternatives_considered: Block the story with a factory_question asking a human to write ACs — defeats autonomous loop
Skip the story entirely — push notifications are in-scope per vision.json
relevant_decisions.rationale: Defined AC-PN-1 (SW registration on app load), AC-PN-2 (enable → permission prompt → subscribe → persist), AC-PN-3 (disable → unsubscribe → mark inactive), AC-PN-4 (blocked state with browser settings hint), AC-PN-5 (upsert notification_preferences on first /settings visit). These map directly to the existing foundation infrastructure (sw.js, /api/push/subscribe, push_subscriptions table, notification_preferences table) and the affected screen (/settings).

relevant_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for push-notifications story
relevant_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
relevant_decisions.rationale: Found: 4 BUILD (lib/notifications/push.ts pure helpers, ServiceWorkerRegistrar, PushNotificationToggle, ensureNotificationPreferences server action), 1 EXTEND (app/(app)/settings/page.tsx — placeholder replaced with real notification sections), 3 REUSE (app/api/push/subscribe/route.ts, public/sw.js push handler, app/api/cron/daily-check/route.ts).

relevant_decisions.decision: ensureNotificationPreferences upserts on /settings page load (not on signup)
relevant_decisions.context: Foundation eval found that cron INNER JOIN on notification_preferences silently excludes users without a prefs row. Options: insert on signup, insert on first /settings visit, or change the JOIN to LEFT JOIN.
alternatives_considered: Insert in signup action — requires modifying auth flow, risk of partial signup if insert fails
Change cron to LEFT JOIN with defaults — loses the ability to store per-user overrides
Insert on first /settings visit (chosen) — lazy creation, zero auth flow changes
relevant_decisions.rationale: Lazy upsert on /settings is the simplest fix: one server action call, race-condition safe (read-then-insert with conflict retry). Users who never visit /settings still get excluded from cron notifications — which is acceptable since they never expressed interest in configuring notifications. The notification-settings story (next in the milestone) will add toggle editing.

relevant_decisions.decision: Builder-defined acceptance criteria for notification-settings (spec was empty)
relevant_decisions.context: state.json story entry for notification-settings has no acceptance_criteria — only id, name, affected_screens (/settings), depends_on (push-notifications). story_context.spec was just {id, name}.
alternatives_considered: Block the story asking for human-written ACs — defeats autonomous loop; the existing /settings page already shows the three boolean prefs + warning days as read-only, making the needed interaction clear
Treat as already done since prefs display on /settings — ignores that they are static text not interactive controls
Add only toggles without the warning-days stepper — leaves order_warning_days uneditable despite being a user-facing config
relevant_decisions.rationale: Defined AC-NS-1 (toggle switches for booleans), AC-NS-2 (stepper for warning days 1-14), AC-NS-3 (optimistic UI with rollback), AC-NS-4 (validation matching DB CHECK). These four criteria map directly to the story name (Notification Preferences) and the /settings screen where the read-only labels already existed from push-notifications story. Each AC has tests and concrete server action calls.

relevant_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for gantt-chart-render
relevant_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
relevant_decisions.rationale: Found: 1 BUILD (GanttChart component + pure computation), 1 EXTEND (/schedule page.tsx to add Gantt rendering + task/dep fetching), 0 REUSE, 0 REFACTOR-THEN-BUILD. Key reuse: existing StageManager stayed in place, types/database.ts Task and TaskDependency types informed the GanttTask shape, stage color palette from StageManager reused in Gantt bars.

relevant_decisions.decision: Pure computation + client component architecture for Gantt chart (no Canvas/SVG-only approach)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-GR-1 requires an interactive Gantt chart. Options: full SVG/Canvas rendering, HTML div-based bars with SVG overlay for arrows, or a third-party Gantt library.
alternatives_considered: Third-party Gantt library (e.g. frappe-gantt, dhtmlx-gantt) — heavy dependencies, hard to style to match the warm earth-tone design, McKinley says no for non-core infra
Full SVG rendering — more control but harder to add interactive features later (tooltips, click handlers, accessibility), slower to implement
Full Canvas rendering — best performance for 1000+ tasks but no DOM accessibility, overkill for typical 20-50 task projects
relevant_decisions.rationale: HTML divs for bars (positioned absolutely, styled with Tailwind) + SVG overlay for dependency arrows. Bars are real DOM elements with title attributes, testable via data-testid, and extensible for click handlers in the gantt-inline-edit story. Pure computation functions in lib/gantt/compute.ts (date range, bar positioning, arrow coordinates) are fully unit-testable. SVG arrows use a simple right-angle path with arrowhead markers.

relevant_decisions.decision: Inclusive-day bar width: widthDays = duration_days (matching existing inclusive-day semantics)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-GR-2 says 'width = duration_days'. The project uses inclusive-day semantics (planned_end = planned_start + duration - 1). A 5-day task starting Apr 1 ends Apr 5 and the bar should span 5 day columns.
alternatives_considered: Exclusive: width = planned_end - planned_start → 4 columns for a 5-day task — misleading visual
Width from dates: daysBetween(planned_start, planned_end) + 1 — correct but redundant since duration_days already encodes this
relevant_decisions.rationale: Using duration_days directly as widthDays is the simplest correct approach, consistent with how tasks were inserted (planned_end = planned_start + duration_days - 1). The Gantt bar visually represents the actual working days.

relevant_decisions.decision: Mobile Gantt shows stages-only accordion (not a scaled-down full Gantt)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-GR-6 specifies 'stages-only view shown by default with horizontal scroll; tapping a stage expands to show its tasks'. Two interpretations: a miniaturized full Gantt with pinch-zoom, or a list of stages that expand to show task bars.
alternatives_considered: Miniaturized full Gantt with pinch-zoom — complex touch handling, bars become unreadable at phone widths
Table/list view with no bars — simpler but loses the visual timeline which is the point of a Gantt
relevant_decisions.rationale: Accordion with per-stage horizontal-scrollable task bars matches the spec literally (stages-only default, tap to expand) and is usable on phone screens. Each expanded stage shows its task bars within a scrollable container at the same DAY_WIDTH as desktop, so the visual language is consistent. Dependency arrows are omitted on mobile for clarity — they add visual noise on small screens and the gantt-inline-edit story is desktop-only anyway.

relevant_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for gantt-zoom-navigate
relevant_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
relevant_decisions.rationale: Found: 1 BUILD (zoom toolbar + config), 2 REUSE (TodayMarker for AC-GZ-2, overflow-x-auto for AC-GZ-3), 1 EXTEND (DateHeader to support weekly/monthly modes). Key reuse: existing TodayMarker component served AC-GZ-2 without modification. Existing overflow-x-auto CSS served AC-GZ-3 without modification. computeTodayOffset shares daysBetween from existing compute.ts.

relevant_decisions.decision: Zoom dayWidth values: week=28, month=8, full=3
relevant_decisions.context: AC-GZ-1 requires three zoom levels. Need to choose pixel-per-day scaling that keeps the chart usable at each level.
alternatives_considered: Logarithmic scaling — complex, harder to reason about bar positioning
Larger month/full values (e.g. 14, 6) — still too wide for year-long projects
Smaller full value (1px) — bars become invisible at low task counts
relevant_decisions.rationale: Week=28 matches original DAY_WIDTH (proven readable for daily labels). Month=8 gives a 365-day project ~2920px width (fits in ~2 scroll lengths on 1440px screen). Full=3 gives a 365-day project ~1095px (fits on screen without scroll). Labels auto-hide below 30px span width to prevent overflow.

relevant_decisions.decision: InteractiveTaskBar uses document-level mousemove/mouseup listeners for drag tracking
relevant_decisions.context: AC-GE-1/AC-GE-2 require drag interactions on Gantt bars. Options: HTML5 drag-and-drop, pointer events with setPointerCapture, or manual mousemove/mouseup on document.
alternatives_considered: HTML5 drag-and-drop: designed for drag-to-target, not pixel-precise resize/move. dragImage snap is jarring.
Pointer capture: cleaner API but less browser support and no natural click-vs-drag threshold.
dnd-kit library: full library for a two-interaction feature; boring tech principle says no new deps for simple drags
relevant_decisions.rationale: Document-level mousemove/mouseup with a 4px threshold to distinguish click from drag. Gives pixel-precise preview updates via React state, works on all desktop browsers, zero new dependencies.

relevant_decisions.decision: Dependency constraint validation runs client-side before server action (AC-GE-4)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-GE-4 requires snapping back to original position on invalid drop. Options: validate client-side and block the server call, or call the server and rollback on error.
alternatives_considered: Server-side validation only: requires a round trip, bar visually jumps then snaps back, feels janky
Both client and server: validate client-side for instant feedback, server validates again as safety net
relevant_decisions.rationale: Client-side check via validateTaskDrop (pure function) gives instant snap-back with no server round trip. The server action still goes through verifyProjectOwnership and cascade_task_dates which would fail on genuine constraint violations. Dependency data is fresh from the server render.

relevant_decisions.decision: Builder-defined acceptance criteria for photo-storage (spec was empty)
relevant_decisions.context: state.json story entry for photo-storage has no acceptance_criteria field — only name, affected_entities (Photo), affected_screens (/tasks/:id, /stages/:id, /photos). story_context.spec was just {id, name}.
alternatives_considered: Block the story with a factory_question asking a human to write ACs — defeats autonomous loop
Skip the story as already covered by storage bucket setup — ignores the UI and upload flow
relevant_decisions.rationale: Defined AC-PS-1 (upload with validation), AC-PS-2 (thumbnails on task detail), AC-PS-3 (gallery grouped by stage), AC-PS-4 (delete removes storage + DB row), AC-PS-5 (client-side file validation). These map directly to the story name and affected screens.

relevant_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for photo-storage
relevant_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
relevant_decisions.rationale: Found: 2 BUILD (lib/photos/operations.ts pure fns, components/photos/TaskPhotosManager.tsx), 2 EXTEND (/tasks/:id page to add photo query + component, /photos page to replace skeleton placeholder), 1 REUSE (existing Photo type from types/database.ts, photos table schema, storage bucket config). Key reuse: Supabase Storage photos bucket already configured with RLS, Photo interface from types/database.ts informed the server action types, createClient/LooseClient pattern from other actions.

relevant_decisions.decision: Signed URLs with 60s expiry for photo previews (not public bucket)
relevant_decisions.context: Photos bucket is private (set during foundation). Options: make bucket public, use signed URLs, or use a proxy route.
alternatives_considered: Make bucket public — violates private storage design from foundation, exposes all project photos
Proxy route (/api/photos/:id) — adds server load for every image request, slower than direct CDN
Long-lived signed URLs (1 hour) — risk of URL sharing/leaking
relevant_decisions.rationale: 60s signed URLs generated server-side in RSC. Photos are only viewable while the page is fresh. Short expiry limits exposure if a URL is shared. Batch generation via createSignedUrls keeps the query count at 1 regardless of photo count.

relevant_decisions.decision: Search Before Building audit for document-storage story
relevant_decisions.context: Pre-implementation code reuse analysis
relevant_decisions.rationale: Found: 0 BUILD (all adapted from existing), 3 EXTEND (server actions, component, pages), 2 REUSE (buildStoragePath pattern, formatFileSize). Key reuse: photo-storage patterns (operations.ts, server actions, component structure) directly adapted for documents. Same Supabase Storage pattern, same project ownership verification, same signed URL approach.

relevant_decisions.decision: Documents render as rows (list) not thumbnails (grid) unlike photos
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DS-2 says each document renders as a row with name, icon, date, download button. Photos use a grid of image thumbnails. Documents are non-visual files — a list layout is more appropriate.
alternatives_considered: Grid layout like photos — wastes space since documents have no visual preview
Table layout — too formal for a mobile-first construction app
Card layout — each document as a card with icon and metadata
relevant_decisions.rationale: AC-DS-2 explicitly says "row" — a compact list with icon + name + date + download button matches the spec and the mobile-first interaction pattern. Each row is a flex container with icon, truncated filename, date/size metadata, download button, and delete button.

relevant_decisions.decision: TaskDocumentsManager is entity-agnostic (works for both tasks and stages)
relevant_decisions.context: AC-DS-1 says documents can be attached from task OR stage detail screen. Options: two components, one component with entity type prop.
alternatives_considered: Separate TaskDocumentsManager and StageDocumentsManager — duplicates 95% of the code
Single component with entityId + entityType props — one source of truth, test once
relevant_decisions.rationale: The upload logic only differs in which FormData field (taskId vs stageId) is populated. A single component with entityType prop eliminates duplication. The stage detail page passes entityType="stage" and the task detail page passes entityType="task".

relevant_decisions.decision: Stage documents query filters task_id IS NULL to avoid showing task-level docs at stage level
relevant_decisions.context: A document attached to a task within a stage has both task_id and stage_id set. On the stage detail page, we only want stage-level documents (no task_id), not documents attached to individual tasks.
alternatives_considered: Show all documents with this stage_id including task-level ones — confusing, user already sees them on each task page
Filter by stage_id AND task_id IS NULL — clean separation of scope
relevant_decisions.rationale: Stage-level documents (e.g., stage permits, stage completion certificates) are conceptually different from task-level documents (e.g., material specs, inspection reports). The IS NULL filter keeps the scope clear.

relevant_decisions.decision: Zero-dependency ZIP builder using STORE method for CSV bundle
relevant_decisions.context: AC-EX-3 requires a .zip archive. No zip library in package.json. Options: add jszip (~45KB), build a minimal zip encoder, or download CSVs individually.
alternatives_considered: Add jszip dependency — another runtime dependency for a single export feature
Download 4 separate CSV files — violates AC-EX-3 which specifies a single .zip archive
Use server-side zip generation — adds server compute cost and a new API route
relevant_decisions.rationale: ZIP STORE format (uncompressed) is a well-documented binary format. The implementation is ~100 lines of pure TypeScript using Uint8Array and TextEncoder — no external deps, fully unit-tested (CRC-32, file headers, EOCD record). CSVs are small text files where compression adds minimal value. Zero-dep = zero supply chain risk for a feature that runs client-side.

relevant_decisions.decision: PDF export via window.print() instead of html2canvas + jspdf
relevant_decisions.context: AC-EX-4 asks for a downloadable PDF of the Gantt chart. The Gantt is a DOM-based component (divs + SVG arrows), not a canvas.
alternatives_considered: Add html2canvas + jspdf (~700KB combined) — capture DOM to canvas then to PDF. Heavy deps, known issues with SVG rendering, adds significant bundle size.
Use puppeteer/playwright server-side screenshot — requires a headless browser runtime on the server, not available in Vercel Functions.
Use @react-pdf/renderer — requires rewriting the Gantt as React-PDF components, massive scope creep.
relevant_decisions.rationale: window.print() is zero-dep, produces high-quality PDF via the browser engine, handles SVG arrows correctly, and works on all target platforms (Chrome, Safari). The user experience is familiar (print dialog → Save as PDF). Logged as a divergence since it opens a dialog rather than auto-downloading.

relevant_decisions.decision: Export data fetched via server action, formatted client-side
relevant_decisions.context: The export flow needs authenticated data access + file generation. Options: server generates the file and returns it, or server returns raw data and client formats.
alternatives_considered: Server-side file generation via API route returning a Blob — requires streaming the response, content-type headers, and a dedicated route per format
Client-side fetch + format — one server action returns all data, client builds JSON/CSV/ZIP using pure functions
relevant_decisions.rationale: One fetchExportData server action returns the complete ExportPayload. Client-side pure functions (buildJsonExport, buildStagesCsv, buildZip) format the data and trigger download via createObjectURL. This keeps the server action simple (data only) and makes the formatting logic fully unit-testable without mocking server infrastructure.

relevant_questions.question: Task edit UI only surfaces duration_days. Name, notes, trade, and depends_on editing for EXISTING tasks is out of scope for task-management (the spec only calls out duration edits in AC-TM-5) — those edits will want a dedicated /tasks/:id edit screen in a later story.
relevant_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — current scope is create + duration-edit + delete. A future task-edit story can extend StageTasksManager or use the /tasks/:id route.

relevant_questions.question: /materials index page (app/(app)/materials/page.tsx) is still the seeded placeholder with pulse skeletons. No AC in material-line-items specifies its content. A follow-up story should define whether it's an ordering queue, a delivery schedule, or an inventory dashboard.
relevant_questions.found_in: spec — affected_screens includes /materials but no AC covers it
relevant_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — left as placeholder; this story only ships the per-task materials UI on /tasks/:id per AC-ML-1 wording

relevant_questions.question: Log Delay entry point on /briefing (AC-DL-1 says '/tasks/:id or /briefing'): satisfied by the /tasks/:id path for this story. /briefing still shows Upcoming Orders only — when the briefing page grows a Today's Tasks section it should link to the per-task delay picker (or embed one inline).
relevant_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — OR satisfied by /tasks/:id. /briefing Log Delay button queued for a daily-briefing follow-up story.

relevant_questions.question: Add Note quick action (AC-QA-3) links to /tasks/:id#notes but no notes editing section exists on the task detail page yet. The task model has a notes field and createTask accepts notes, but there is no inline edit-notes UI on /tasks/:id. A future story should add an editable notes section.
relevant_questions.resolved_as: Link to /tasks/:id#notes — the notes editing section will be added in a future task-edit story. The quick action is functional as navigation.

relevant_questions.question: /stages/:id page does not yet show a photos section. The affected_screens for photo-storage includes /stages/:id but only /tasks/:id has the upload UI. A future story could add a StagePhotosManager to /stages/:id that aggregates photos from all tasks in that stage.
relevant_questions.resolved_as: Deferred — /photos gallery already groups by stage, and /tasks/:id is the primary upload surface. Per-stage photo section can be added when stage detail pages get richer.
